difficult.name: test_4_3_2_vote_request_rejects_stale_epoch
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/voteRequest rejects a candidate with stale candidate_epoch (granted=false).

difficult.name: test_4_3_2_vote_request_grants_same_candidate_same_epoch
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/voteRequest is idempotent for the already-voted candidate in the current epoch (granted=true).

difficult.name: test_4_3_2_vote_request_rejects_different_candidate_same_epoch
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/voteRequest rejects a second different candidate in the same epoch after voting (granted=false).

difficult.name: test_4_3_2_vote_request_grants_vote_in_next_epoch
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/voteRequest grants a vote for a candidate in the current epoch+1 (granted=true).

difficult.name: test_4_3_3_begin_quorum_epoch_accepts_current_state
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/beginQuorumEpoch accepts the current leader/epoch already stored in quorum-state (accepted=true).

difficult.name: test_4_3_3_begin_quorum_epoch_accepts_current_state
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/beginQuorumEpoch accepts the current leader/epoch already stored in quorum-state (accepted=true).

difficult.name: test_4_3_3_begin_quorum_epoch_rejects_fenced_epoch
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/beginQuorumEpoch rejects a fenced leader_epoch (accepted=false).

difficult.name: test_4_3_3_begin_quorum_epoch_rejects_fenced_epoch
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/beginQuorumEpoch rejects a fenced leader_epoch (accepted=false).

difficult.name: test_4_3_3_begin_quorum_epoch_rejects_conflicting_leader_same_epoch
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/beginQuorumEpoch rejects a conflicting leader_id for the same leader_epoch (accepted=false).

difficult.name: test_4_3_3_begin_quorum_epoch_rejects_conflicting_leader_same_epoch
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/beginQuorumEpoch rejects a conflicting leader_id for the same leader_epoch (accepted=false).

difficult.name: test_4_3_4_cluster_elects_stable_leader
difficult.description: After startup, all brokers converge on a stable leader (leader_broker_id != -1 and consistent).

difficult.name: test_4_3_5_fetch_metadata_200_leader_has_records
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/fetchMetadata returns 200 on the leader and includes a 'records' list.

difficult.name: test_4_3_5_fetch_metadata_421_follower_rejects
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/fetchMetadata returns 421 when called on a follower (not the leader).

difficult.name: test_4_3_5_fetch_metadata_includes_created_topic
difficult.description: POST /kraft/v1/fetchMetadata on the leader includes the topic_name of a recently created topic.

difficult.name: test_4_3_5_metadata_replicated_to_all_followers
difficult.description: All non-leader brokers replicate the metadata log entry for a created topic (topic_name appears in __cluster_metadata.log).

difficult.name: test_4_3_8_election_after_leader_failure
difficult.description: When the current leader broker is stopped, the remaining brokers elect a new stable leader.

difficult.name: test_4_3_9_follower_recovery_does_not_change_leader
difficult.description: When a follower broker is stopped and restarted, it rejoins without triggering a leader change.

        """
        Poll the broker container filesystem until the produced payload is present in any partition .log file.
    
        This is used for exercise 4.2.3 replication checks. We search for a directory matching:
        */data/<topic_partition>
        and then scan any '*.log' files under it. We use `grep -a -F` to handle both text and binary logs.
        """
        host_port = _host_port_from_url(broker_url)

            "[ -n \"$dir\" ] || exit 2; "
            # Scan all segment logs; -a treats binary as text, -F is fixed string.
            "cat \"$dir\"/*.log 2>/dev/null | grep -a -F -q -- \"$pl\""
        )
    
        while time.time() < deadline:
